Convert the tax liability for the art collection by first applying the 20% tax rate to its appraised value of $2 million. Convert this amount to $400,000. Apply the 5% discount on the taxable amount, which reduces the tax by 5% of $400,000, resulting in a $20,000 reduction. Convert the adjusted tax liability for the art collection to $380,000.

Convert the real estate portfolio tax calculation by dividing the $5 million value into the specified brackets. Convert the first $1 million to a 10% tax, resulting in $100,000. Convert the next $2 million to a 15% tax, resulting in $300,000. Convert the remaining $2 million to a 20% tax, resulting in $400,000. Convert the total tax liability for the real estate portfolio to $800,000.

Convert the securities portfolio tax liability by subtracting the $100,000 deduction from the $3 million value, resulting in a taxable amount of $2.9 million. Convert the 15% tax rate applied to this amount to $435,000. Convert the total tax liability for the securities portfolio to $435,000.

Convert the total tax liability for the entire estate by summing the liabilities from all three assets. Convert the total to $380,000 (art) + $800,000 (real estate) + $435,000 (securities) = $1,615,000.

Convert the misvaluation error in the real estate portfolio by increasing its value by 10%. Convert the original $5 million to $5.5 million. Convert the new value into the tax brackets: the first $1 million is taxed at 10%, resulting in $100,000. Convert the next $2 million to a 15% tax, resulting in $300,000. Convert the remaining $2.5 million to a 20% tax, resulting in $500,000. Convert the total tax liability for the real estate portfolio to $900,000.
